subject: DevOps
title: The Assembly Line Metaphor
description: A tiny lesson with a big payoff: The Assembly Line Metaphor.

hook.text: Henry Ford's assembly line revolutionized manufacturing. CI/CD pipelines do the same for software.

buildup.text: On a factory line, each station does one job: weld, paint, inspect. The car moves forward automatically.

discovery.text: In a pipeline, each stage does one job: compile, test, scan, deploy. Code moves forward automatically.

twist.text: If any station fails, the line stops. Same with pipelines — a failed test halts the deploy instantly.

climax.text: The metaphor breaks in one way: software pipelines run in minutes, not hours. Digital beats physical.

punchline.text: Software assembly lines: each stage adds quality, not parts.

quiz.question: What happens in a CI/CD pipeline when a test stage fails?
options: The pipeline stops and blocks the deployment
The code deploys anyway with a warning
